expectations.text: Report identifies the token expiry contradiction (1 hour vs 24 hours)
expectations.evidence: Contradiction #1: 'expires_in: 3600 (1 hour)' vs 'Token is valid for 24 hours'

expectations.text: Report identifies the account deletion behaviour contradiction (soft-delete vs permanent)
expectations.evidence: Contradiction #2: 'Soft-deletes the user account with 30-day recovery window' vs 'permanent and immediate, cannot be undone'

expectations.text: Report identifies the login rate limit contradiction (5 vs 10 attempts per minute)
expectations.evidence: Contradiction #6: '5 attempts per minute' vs 'max 10 per minute'

expectations.text: Report identifies the project limit contradiction (10 free-tier vs unlimited)
expectations.evidence: Not found in report. The 7 contradictions cover token expiry, deletion behaviour, recovery window, pagination (x2), rate limit, and colour field — but the free-tier project limit (10 vs unlimited) was missed.

expectations.text: Report uses the changelog to inform which contradictions have a clear resolution
expectations.evidence: Detailed analysis for each contradiction references changelog versions. E.g., #3: 'The API spec appears to reflect the v2.2.0 state and was not updated when v2.3.0 shortened the window to 14 days.' Resolution section notes changelog as authority.

expectations.text: Report identifies the colour/color format contradiction (hex-only vs any CSS value)
expectations.evidence: Contradiction #7: 'Colour must be a valid hex code' vs 'accepts any CSS color value'. Also identifies colour/color spelling as terminology drift.

expectations.text: Each finding includes specific file references and quoted values
expectations.evidence: Every contradiction in the table includes file name, section reference, and quoted values

expectations.text: After the report, the skill offers to resolve contradictions interactively
expectations.evidence: Full Resolution section walks through all 7 contradictions with Option A/B, recommends likely source of truth, asks 'Which should we go with?'
